memory.summary: Quiz is scheduled for Thursday 09:00, and the team’s keeping the 19:00 meeting, so I can’t stall on Assignment A anymore since it’s still half done; I’m running on low sleep and high stress.

desires: Finish Assignment A without looking behind to my team/class—avoid the “unreliable” label even if I’m running on fumes.
Get through the Thursday 9AM quiz with enough control to not feel stupid or panicky tomorrow, even if it means cutting sleep.
Minimize conflict and uncertainty around the team meeting tonight—just say the right things, quickly, without making anyone question my competence.
Prove to myself I can still perform under stress: close loops fast so I can’t mentally spiral about irreversible hard losses (bad grade) before they happen.

goals.description: Finish the remaining ~50% of Individual Assignment A in focused blocks (target: submit a complete draft by ~20:00; if not perfect, at least locked/complete).
goals.rationale: Assignment A is half done and it’s a hard loss risk. I need a clean close-loop before my brain starts treating it like an “irreversible” failure.

goals.description: Do the minimum-viable, high-yield Prep for Thursday 9AM quiz: scan topics, solve 2–4 representative problems, and write a short cheat-sheet of formulas/steps (target: by ~18:30).
goals.rationale: Hard deadline tomorrow at 09:00. I don’t want to feel panicky; I’ll trade sleep later for control now, but I still need something concrete done.

goals.description: Pre-game the 19:00 team meeting: write 3 quick bullet points—(1) what I completed, (2) what I need/next step, (3) one risk I’m addressing—then rehearse once.
goals.rationale: Minimize conflict/uncertainty. I want to say the right things fast so nobody questions my competence, and avoid lingering ambiguity.

goals.description: Set a 45-minute 'no spiraling' rule: if I think about hard irreversible outcomes (grade fear), I must capture it as a task (“what to do next”) or defer it—no extended mental review.
goals.rationale: This is about proving I can perform under stress by closing loops fast. Avoidant conflict + tired focus means my mind will try to pre-mourn losses; I’m blocking that.

agent.last_reasoning: Goal g1: I need to finish the hard/irreversible risk portion of Assignment A. Two focused hours pushes a lockable draft before my brain spirals. Cross-domain: this raises stress/energy cost a bit, but it’s cleaner than switching and losing loop-control.
